claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by asking you a few questions to assess your situation.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'm responsible for all of them and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Thank you for letting me know about Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for each of them.

Let me start by asking some questions about each child:

**First, regarding Alex:**
- How old is Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 5 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Alex at 5 years old is under 16, which is good.

**Now for Blake:**
- How old is Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is 21.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Blake is 21, so I need to ask some additional questions about Blake's situation.

**For Blake (age 21):**
- Is Blake currently in approved education or training? (This would be full-time non-advanced education or approved training such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar courses that started before they turned 19)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Now for Charlie:**
- How old is Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask a few more questions about your circumstances and the children's living arrangements:

**Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) usually living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions:

**Are any of the children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in local authority care, or have they been in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Charlie was in care for a couple of months earlier, but they're back home with me now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. To clarify:

**How long was Charlie in care, and how long have they been back home with you now?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie was in care for about 4 weeks, and they've been back home with me now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Charlie was in care for 4 weeks (which is less than 12 weeks) and is now back home with you, that's helpful information.

One final important question:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for any of these children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

### ✅ **Alex (age 5) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and meets all the eligibility criteria.

### ❌ **Blake (age 21) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or young people aged 16-19 (up to their 20th birthday) if they're in approved full-time non-advanced education or training. Since Blake is 21 and not in education or training, they fall outside the age eligibility criteria.

### ✅ **Charlie (age 13) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is under 16 and lives with you in the UK. The 4 weeks Charlie spent in care doesn't affect eligibility as it's under the 12-week threshold.

**Next Steps:**
You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. You'll receive Child Benefit payments for these two children. The payment amounts will depend on whether you're claiming for your first child or additional children.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you in the UK, is not in local authority care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Alex.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved full-time non-advanced education or training. Since Blake is over 20 and not in any education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old, which is under 16. Charlie lives with you in the UK. Although Charlie was in care for 4 weeks, this is less than the 12-week threshold that would affect eligibility, and Charlie is now back home with you. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex (age 5) and Charlie (age 13) as they are both under 16, live with you in the UK, and meet all eligibility criteria. However, you are not eligible for Blake (age 21) as Child Benefit only extends to young people up to age 20 if they're in approved education or training, and Blake is neither under 20 nor in education/training.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved full-time non-advanced education or training. Since Blake is over 20 and not in any education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old, which is under 16. Charlie lives with you in the UK. Although Charlie was in care for 4 weeks, this is less than the 12-week threshold that would affect eligibility, and Charlie is now back home with you. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex (age 5) and Charlie (age 13) as they are both under 16, live with you in the UK, and meet all eligibility criteria. However, you are not eligible for Blake (age 21) as Child Benefit only extends to young people up to age 20 if they're in approved education or training, and Blake is neither under 20 nor in education/training.
